name: BaseTable
description: A low-level, headless table renderer built on top of TanStack Table v8. Renders a semantic HTML table with full control over every section (thead, tbody, tfoot), supports row virtualization, row reordering via drag-and-drop, column pinning, sticky header/footer, and group headers. All styling and behavior is delegated to the consumer via render props.
when_to_use: When you need full control over table markup and styling without the opinionated sizing/alignment layer that Table adds.
When building custom table compositions that require custom header, footer, or cell rendering.
When integrating row virtualization for large datasets.
When implementing drag-and-drop row reordering together with ReorderingProvider.
avoid: Avoid using directly when a styled table with standard size and alignment variants is sufficient — use Table instead.
Avoid using without calling useTable first; the `table` prop expects the TanStack Table instance.

props.name: table
props.description: The table instance returned from the useTable hook.

props.name: attributes
props.description: HTML attributes for the <table> element.

props.name: bodyAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for the <tbody> element.

props.name: bodyClassName
props.description: CSS class for the <tbody> element.

props.name: bodyRef
props.description: Ref for the <tbody> element.

props.name: cellAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for <td> elements inside <tbody>.

props.name: cellClassName
props.description: CSS class for <td> elements inside <tbody>.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the <table> element.

props.name: customFooterRowCount
props.description: Used with renderCustomFooterContent to set the correct aria-rowcount.

props.name: emptyContent
props.description: Content displayed when the table has no data rows.

props.name: footerAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for the <tfoot> element.

props.name: footerCellAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for <th> elements inside <tfoot>.

props.name: footerCellClassName
props.description: CSS class for <th> elements inside <tfoot>.

props.name: footerClassName
props.description: CSS class for the <tfoot> element.

props.name: footerRowAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for <tr> elements inside <tfoot>.

props.name: footerRowClassName
props.description: CSS class for <tr> elements inside <tfoot>.

props.name: getGroupTitle
props.description: Returns the title for a group header row.

props.name: getIsCustomRow
props.description: Checks if the row should be rendered using renderCustomRowContent.

props.name: getIsGroupHeaderRow
props.description: Checks if the row should be rendered as a group header.

props.name: groupHeaderClassName
props.description: CSS class for group header elements.

props.name: headerAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for the <thead> element.

props.name: headerCellAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for <th> elements inside <thead>.

props.name: headerCellClassName
props.type: string | ((header: Header<TData, TValue>, parentHeader?: Header<TData, unknown>) => string)
props.description: CSS class for <th> elements inside <thead>.

props.name: headerClassName
props.description: CSS class for the <thead> element.

props.name: headerRowAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for <tr> elements inside <thead>.

props.name: headerRowClassName
props.description: CSS class for <tr> elements inside <thead>.

props.name: onRowClick
props.description: Click handler for rows inside <tbody>.

props.name: renderCustomFooterContent
props.type: (props: {cellClassName: string; footerGroups: HeaderGroup<TData>[]; rowClassName: string; rowIndex: number}) => React.ReactNode
props.description: Function to render custom footer content.

props.name: renderCustomRowContent
props.description: Function to render custom rows when getIsCustomRow returns true.

props.name: renderGroupHeader
props.type: (props: {row: Row<TData>; className?: string; getGroupTitle?: (row: Row<TData>) => React.ReactNode}) => React.ReactNode
props.description: Function to override the default rendering of group headers.

props.name: renderGroupHeaderRowContent
props.type: (props: {row: Row<TData>; Cell: React.FunctionComponent<BaseCellProps<TData>>; cellClassName?: string; getGroupTitle?: (row: Row<TData>) => React.ReactNode}) => React.ReactNode
props.description: Function to override the default rendering of the entire group header row.

props.name: renderHeaderCellContent
props.description: Function to override the default rendering of header cell content.

props.name: renderResizeHandle
props.description: Function to override the default rendering of resize handles.

props.name: renderSortIndicator
props.description: Function to override the default rendering of sort indicators.

props.name: resizeHandleClassName
props.description: CSS class for resize handles in <th> elements.

props.name: rowAttributes
props.description: HTML attributes for <tr> elements inside <tbody>.

props.name: rowClassName
props.description: CSS class for <tr> elements inside <tbody>.

props.name: rowVirtualizer
props.description: The row virtualizer instance from useRowVirtualizer or useWindowRowVirtualizer.

props.name: sortIndicatorClassName
props.description: CSS class for the sort indicator inside <th> elements.

props.name: stickyFooter
props.description: Makes the <tfoot> element sticky.

props.name: stickyHeader
props.description: Makes the <thead> element sticky.

props.name: withFooter
props.description: Determines whether the <tfoot> element should be rendered.

props.name: withHeader
props.description: Determines whether the <thead> element should be rendered.

examples.title: Basic table
Table with row selection
Virtualized table

name: DragHandle
description: A drag handle icon button for table rows that integrates with dnd-kit's useSortable. Renders a grip icon and exposes dnd-kit drag listeners and attributes on the span element. Used inside the dragHandleColumn column definition.
when_to_use: When building a custom drag handle cell for row reordering instead of using the pre-built dragHandleColumn constant.
When you need to customize the drag handle appearance beyond the default grip icon.
avoid: Avoid using outside of a dnd-kit DndContext and SortableContext — it relies on useSortable internally.
Avoid using without ReorderingProvider wrapping the table; use dragHandleColumn constant for standard cases.

props.name: row
props.description: The TanStack Table row object. Its id is used as the dnd-kit sortable id.

examples.title: Custom drag handle cell renderer

name: RangedSelectionCheckbox
description: A checkbox component for table row selection that supports shift-click range selection. Wraps the Gravity UI Checkbox and uses useToggleRangeSelectionHandler internally to enable selecting a contiguous range of rows when holding Shift.
when_to_use: When you need a selection checkbox in a table cell that supports shift+click to select a range of rows.
Use inside a custom selection column cell renderer where range selection behavior is required.
avoid: Avoid using outside of a table cell context — it requires a CellContext prop from TanStack Table.
Avoid using for simple single-row selection without a table; use Gravity UI Checkbox directly instead.

props.name: cellContext
props.description: The TanStack Table cell context object, used to determine row selection state and provide table access for range selection.

props.name: checked
props.description: Whether the checkbox is checked.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the checkbox is disabled.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: Whether the checkbox is in an indeterminate state.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the checkbox root element.

examples.title: Custom selection column with range selection

name: ReorderingProvider
description: A context provider that enables drag-and-drop row reordering in a table. Wraps children with dnd-kit DndContext and SortableContext, using the table's row ids as sortable items. Supports optional row nesting via drag depth detection.
when_to_use: When you need to allow users to reorder table rows by dragging.
When implementing tree/nested row reordering with drag depth nesting support.
Wrap BaseTable or Table with this provider along with the dragHandleColumn.
avoid: Avoid using without the dragHandleColumn or a custom drag handle in the table columns — users need a drag target.
Avoid nesting multiple ReorderingProviders; use a single provider per table.

props.name: table
props.description: The table instance returned from useTable.

props.name: onReorder
props.description: Callback triggered when a drag ends. Receives drag result with draggedItemKey, baseItemKey, and nesting info.

props.name: children
props.description: The table component to render inside the dnd context.

props.name: dndModifiers
props.description: A list of dnd-kit modifiers to apply to the DndContext.

props.name: enableNesting
props.description: Determines whether elements can be nested using drag-and-drop (horizontal drag gesture changes depth).

examples.title: Basic row reordering

name: SortIndicator
description: A sort direction indicator icon with an ActionTooltip describing the next sort action. Renders the appropriate arrow icon based on the column's current sort state (ascending, descending, or unsorted). Designed to be used inside table header cells.
when_to_use: Use inside a custom header cell renderer to display the current sort order for a column.
Use as the value for BaseTable's renderSortIndicator prop to replace the default sort indicator.
avoid: Avoid using outside of a TanStack Table header context — it requires a header prop with column sort methods.
Avoid using for non-table sorting UI; use a standalone icon or button instead.

props.name: header
props.description: The TanStack Table header object for the column being sorted.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the sort indicator span element.

examples.title: Custom sort indicator in header cell

name: Table
description: A styled table component built on top of BaseTable that adds Gravity UI design tokens for size (s | m), vertical alignment of cells and headers, and interactive row styling. Extends all BaseTable props with size, verticalAlign, headerVerticalAlign, and footerVerticalAlign.
when_to_use: Use as the primary table component for most product UIs requiring Gravity UI design consistency.
When you need built-in size variants (s/m) and vertical alignment without writing custom styles.
When onRowClick is needed with automatic interactive row styling.
avoid: Avoid when you need completely custom cell/row markup that conflicts with the applied CSS class modifiers — use BaseTable instead.
Avoid using without the useTable hook; the table prop is required.

props.name: table
props.description: The table instance returned from useTable.

props.name: size
props.description: Table size variant controlling cell padding and row height.

props.name: verticalAlign
props.description: Vertical alignment for body cells.

props.name: headerVerticalAlign
props.description: Vertical alignment for header cells.

props.name: footerVerticalAlign
props.description: Vertical alignment for footer cells.

props.name: onRowClick
props.description: Click handler for body rows. Automatically applies interactive styling to rows when set.

examples.title: Basic styled table
Table with row click and size S

name: TableSettings
description: A column visibility and order management UI rendered as a gear-icon button that opens a popup. Supports drag-and-drop column reordering (via dnd-kit), column visibility toggles, optional search filtering of columns, and nested column group expansion. Applies changes back to the TanStack Table instance on confirm.
when_to_use: When users need to show/hide or reorder table columns at runtime.
Embed in a table header cell using getSettingsColumn or getSettingsWithActionsColumn helpers.
When you have many columns and need search to find them quickly via enableSearch.
avoid: Avoid when the column set is fixed and users should not modify it.
Avoid using without useTableSettings to manage visibility and order state — the table instance needs proper state wiring.

props.name: table
props.description: The table instance returned from useTable.

props.name: sortable
props.description: Enables drag-and-drop column reordering inside the settings popup.

props.name: filterable
props.description: Enables column visibility toggles (show/hide checkboxes).

props.name: enableSearch
props.description: Adds a search input to filter columns by name inside the popup.

props.name: searchPlaceholder
props.description: Placeholder text for the search input when enableSearch is true.

props.name: onSettingsApply
props.description: Callback fired when the user applies settings, receiving the new visibility and order state.

examples.title: TableSettings with useTableSettings
Embedded in column header via getSettingsColumn

name: TreeExpandableCell
description: A cell component that renders a toggle button for expanding or collapsing tree rows. Displays an ArrowToggle icon that reflects the current expanded state of the row, and wraps children (the actual cell content) in a flex container alongside the toggle.
when_to_use: Use inside a cell renderer for the name/primary column of a tree table to provide expand/collapse controls.
When rendering hierarchical data where rows have sub-rows managed by TanStack Table's expanding feature.
avoid: Avoid using for flat tables without a tree/nested row structure.
Avoid using as a standalone expand button outside of a TanStack Table cell context.

props.name: row
props.description: The TanStack Table row object used to determine expandability and current expanded state.

props.name: children
props.description: The cell content rendered alongside the expand toggle button.

examples.title: Tree name column with expandable cell

name: experimentalUseColumnsAutoSize
description: Automatically calculates optimal column widths by measuring header and cell content in an invisible container. Supports React nodes, primitive values, accessorKey/accessorFn columns, user resizing, and predefined widths. Returns auto-sized column definitions and a table instance setter.
when_to_use: When column widths should adapt to content without manual configuration.
When combining with column resizing so user-resized widths are preserved.
For tables where content width varies significantly across rows.
avoid: Avoid using on tables with extremely large datasets without adjusting sampleSize — measuring is expensive.
This hook is experimental; avoid using in production if API stability is required.
signature: function experimentalUseColumnsAutoSize<TData>(props: UseColumnsAutoSizeProps<TData>): { columnWidths: Record<string, number>; setTableInstance: (table: ReturnType<typeof useTable<TData>>) => void; columnsWithAutoSizes: ColumnDef<TData>[]; isMeasuring: boolean; }

parameters.name: columns
parameters.description: The column definitions for your table.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Configuration: minWidth (50), maxWidth (500), padding (16), headerPadding (24), sampleSize (100), measureHeaderText (true), respectExistingWidths (true), respectResizedWidths (true).

parameters.name: renderElementForMeasure
parameters.description: Custom renderer wrapping measured elements — use to inject context providers (e.g. Redux Provider).

return_type: { columnWidths: Record<string, number>; setTableInstance: (table) => void; columnsWithAutoSizes: ColumnDef<TData>[]; isMeasuring: boolean; }

name: useDraggableRowDepth
description: Computes the visual depth and first-child status of a row being dragged in a nested/tree reordering scenario. Reads the SortableListContext to determine the current drag position and horizontal offset mode (child, parent, or next-child), returning the depth the row would land at if dropped.
when_to_use: Use inside a custom draggable row renderer when building tree reordering with visual depth feedback.
When you need to show a preview of the row's new nesting level during drag.
avoid: Avoid using for flat (non-nested) tables where row depth is always 0.
Avoid using outside of a ReorderingProvider context — it depends on SortableListContext.
signature: function useDraggableRowDepth<TData>(options: UseDraggableRowDepthOptions<TData>): { depth: number; isFirstChild: boolean; }

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: The row being dragged.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: The table instance.

parameters.name: isDragging
parameters.description: Whether this row is currently being dragged.

name: useRowVirtualizer
description: A thin wrapper around TanStack Virtual's useVirtualizer for virtualizing table rows inside a scroll container element. Returns a virtualizer instance to pass to BaseTable or Table via the rowVirtualizer prop.
when_to_use: When rendering tables with hundreds or thousands of rows where DOM performance is a concern.
When the scroll container is a specific DOM element (not the window) — use useWindowRowVirtualizer for window-level scrolling.
avoid: Avoid using for small datasets (< ~100 rows) where virtualization overhead is unnecessary.
Do not use when the scroll container is the browser window — use useWindowRowVirtualizer instead.
signature: function useRowVirtualizer<TScrollElement extends Element>(options: UseRowVirtualizerOptions<TScrollElement>): Virtualizer<TScrollElement, HTMLTableRowElement>

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: TanStack Virtual useVirtualizer options: count, estimateSize, getScrollElement, overscan, etc.

name: useTable
description: A wrapper around TanStack Table's useReactTable that pre-configures sensible defaults for all feature flags (sorting, filtering, grouping, expanding, selection, column pinning, resizing). Automatically wires the appropriate row/sort/filter/group models based on which features are enabled.
when_to_use: Use as the primary way to create a table instance for all table components in this library.
When you need a TanStack Table instance with Gravity UI table's extended ColumnDef type that supports withNestingStyles and showTreeDepthIndicators.
avoid: Avoid calling useReactTable directly — useTable provides correct defaults and extended types.
Avoid setting getCoreRowModel manually unless you have a custom model; it is provided by default.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: All TanStack Table options with Gravity UI's extended ColumnDef type. Feature flags default to false. getCoreRowModel is optional.

name: useTableSettings
description: Manages column visibility and column order state for use with TableSettings and useTable. Returns the state object and callbacks to wire into useTable's state and event handlers, with optional callbacks for external persistence.
when_to_use: Use alongside TableSettings component to manage which columns are visible and in what order.
When you need to persist column visibility or order changes via onVisibilityChange / onOrderingChange callbacks.
avoid: Avoid using without passing the returned state and callbacks to useTable — the table instance must be aware of these states.
Avoid using when column visibility and order are static and never changed by users.
signature: function useTableSettings(options?: UseTableSettingsOptions): { state: { columnVisibility: VisibilityState; columnOrder: string[] }; callbacks: { onColumnVisibilityChange: (value: Updater<VisibilityState>) => void; onColumnOrderChange: (value: Updater<string[]>) => void; } }

parameters.name: initialOrdering
parameters.description: Initial column order array.

parameters.name: initialVisibility
parameters.description: Initial column visibility state (record of columnId -> boolean).

parameters.name: onOrderingChange
parameters.description: Callback fired when column order changes.

parameters.name: onVisibilityChange
parameters.description: Callback fired when column visibility changes.

return_type: { state: { columnVisibility: VisibilityState; columnOrder: string[] }; callbacks: { onColumnVisibilityChange, onColumnOrderChange } }

name: useToggleRangeSelectionHandler
description: Returns a checkbox onChange handler that supports shift+click range selection for table rows. When shift is held and a previous row was selected, all rows in the range are toggled. Falls back to single row toggle for nested tables or when shift is not held.
when_to_use: Use inside a custom selection cell renderer when you want shift+click range selection behavior.
Used internally by RangedSelectionCheckbox — use that component instead for most cases.
avoid: Avoid using outside of a table cell context — it requires a CellContext from TanStack Table.
Avoid using for tree/nested tables — range selection is automatically disabled for tables with sub-rows.
signature: function useToggleRangeSelectionHandler<TData, TValue>(cellContext: CellContext<TData, TValue>): (event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void

parameters.name: cellContext
parameters.description: The TanStack Table cell context from the cell renderer.

name: useWindowRowVirtualizer
description: A thin wrapper around TanStack Virtual's useWindowVirtualizer for virtualizing table rows when the scroll container is the browser window. Returns a virtualizer instance to pass to BaseTable or Table via the rowVirtualizer prop.
when_to_use: When the page itself scrolls (not a fixed-height container) and you need row virtualization.
For full-page table layouts where the browser window is the scroll container.
avoid: Avoid using when the scroll container is a specific DOM element — use useRowVirtualizer instead.
Avoid using for small datasets where virtualization is unnecessary.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: TanStack Virtual useWindowVirtualizer options: count, estimateSize, overscan, etc.

name: dragHandleColumn
description: A pre-built TanStack Table column definition for a drag handle cell. Renders a DragHandle component in each body cell and has a blank header. Has a fixed size of 32px and sets hideInSettings: true in meta so it does not appear in TableSettings.
when_to_use: Prepend to your columns array when enabling row reordering with ReorderingProvider.
When you want a standard drag handle without building a custom column definition.
avoid: Avoid using without wrapping the table in ReorderingProvider — the drag handle will not function.
Avoid using when row reordering is not needed.

name: getActionsColumn
description: Returns a TanStack Table column definition that renders a row actions menu (ellipsis button with dropdown) in each body cell. Accepts getRowActions for menu item configuration or renderRowActions for fully custom rendering. The column id defaults to '_actions' and is hidden from TableSettings.
when_to_use: When each table row needs contextual actions (edit, delete, etc.) in a dropdown menu.
Use getRowActions for standard action items or renderRowActions for custom action UI.
avoid: Avoid using when actions are inline in regular cells rather than a separate actions column.
Avoid passing both getRowActions and renderRowActions — prefer one approach.
signature: function getActionsColumn<TValue>(columnId?: string, options: TableActionsSettings<TValue>): ColumnDef<TValue>

parameters.name: columnId
parameters.description: Custom column id. Defaults to ACTIONS_COLUMN_ID ('_actions').

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: getRowActions, renderRowActions, and rowActionsSize configuration.

name: getSettingsColumn
description: Returns a TanStack Table column definition that renders the TableSettings gear button in the header cell. Used to embed column settings management directly in the table header. The column has a fixed size of 44px and is hidden from the settings popup itself.
when_to_use: When you want the TableSettings popup trigger to live inside the table's last header cell.
Append to your columns array to get a standard settings column without manual column definition.
avoid: Avoid using without useTableSettings to manage the visibility/order state that TableSettings applies.
Avoid when the settings button should live outside the table header.
signature: function getSettingsColumn<TData>(columnId?: string, options?: TableSettingsOptions): ColumnDef<TData>

parameters.name: columnId
parameters.description: Custom column id. Defaults to SETTINGS_COLUMN_ID ('_settings').

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Options passed to the TableSettings component: sortable, filterable, enableSearch, searchPlaceholder.

name: getSettingsWithActionsColumn
description: Returns a combined TanStack Table column definition that shows the TableSettings gear button in the header and a row actions menu in the body cells. Combines getSettingsColumn and getActionsColumn into a single 44px column to save horizontal space.
when_to_use: When you need both column settings management and row actions in the same column to conserve table width.
Use as a single column instead of separate getSettingsColumn and getActionsColumn when space is limited.
avoid: Avoid using when settings and actions UI would be confusing in a single column — consider separate columns.
Avoid using without the actions options; use getSettingsColumn alone if no row actions are needed.
signature: function getSettingsWithActionsColumn<TData>(columnId?: string, options: { actions: TableActionsSettings<TData>; settings?: TableSettingsOptions }): ColumnDef<TData>

parameters.name: columnId
parameters.description: Custom column id. Defaults to SETTINGS_WITH_ACTIONS_COLUMN_ID ('_settings-with-actions').

parameters.name: options.actions
parameters.description: Row actions configuration: getRowActions, renderRowActions, rowActionsSize.

parameters.name: options.settings
parameters.description: TableSettings options: sortable, filterable, enableSearch, searchPlaceholder.

name: getVirtualRowRangeExtractor
description: Returns a custom range extractor function for TanStack Virtual that ensures pinned rows and the currently dragging row remain in the rendered range during virtualization. Pass to useRowVirtualizer's rangeExtractor option.
when_to_use: When using row virtualization together with row pinning or drag-and-drop reordering, to keep those rows visible.
Pass to useRowVirtualizer({ rangeExtractor: getVirtualRowRangeExtractor(...) }).
avoid: Avoid using when row pinning and drag reordering are not used alongside virtualization.

name: selectionColumn
description: A pre-built TanStack Table column definition for row selection. Renders a SelectionCheckbox in the header (select all) and a RangedSelectionCheckbox in each body cell (individual row selection with shift+click range support). Has a fixed size of 32px and is hidden from TableSettings.
when_to_use: Prepend to your columns array when enabling row selection in a table.
Provides select-all header checkbox and per-row checkboxes with range selection out of the box.
avoid: Avoid using without enabling enableRowSelection in useTable options — checkboxes will be disabled.
Avoid using for single-select scenarios where radio-button behavior is preferred.
